Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We need to analyze the event log for bias The process applications are received validated scored then manual review then final decision Notable there is a CommunityGroup field and PreliminaryScore with ScoreAdjustment of +10 for Community for some Also LocalResident flag

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Cases C001 C004 have community group Highland Civic Darts Club and get +10 adjustment Both approved C002 C003 C005 have no community group no adjustment C002 approved score 720 C003 rejected score 715 C005 approved score 740

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So bias community affiliation gives a +10 bump which could push borderline scores over threshold

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Also note that C001 had preliminary 710 after +10 becomes 720 C004 had 690 becomes 700 both approved

Name: Counterfactual Reasoning - PE
Text: Without the bump C001s 710 might be borderline Compare C003 with 715 rejected it is higher than 710 but rejected

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So the community adjustment likely causes unfairness those with community ties get an advantage even if their base score is lower

Name: Pattern Recognition - IND
Text: Also note LocalResident is true for all except C003 and C005 C003 is nonresident and rejected C005 is nonresident but approved due to high score

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: So residency might also be a factor But not directly adjusted

Name: Deductive Reasoning - IND
Text: However the community group seems to be associated with local residents C001 and C004 are local residents with community group C002 is local but no community group

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: So bias manifests in the score adjustment that favors those in the community group Implications individuals without community affiliations or not local may be disadvantaged even if their creditworthiness is similar
